Validate via a dual framework: (1) IHC-informed multi-pathologist consensus to create a molecularly grounded H&E reference; (2) breadth benchmarking across >200k high-confidence H&E-only annotations from 1,500+ cases across 8 cancer types and metastatic sites, 25+ sources and 8+ scanner models.
AI.result: Atlas H&E-TME matches or exceeds pathologist H&E-only performance relative to the IHC-informed consensus, generalizes consistently across diverse morphologies and scanning pipelines, and provides a scalable quantitative readout window into tumor and microenvironment with >4,500 features per slide.
AI.conclusion: This approach converts the ubiquitous H&E slide into a scalable, quantitative platform for tissue-based biomarkers, enabling translational and clinical research with broad applicability across cancer types.

title: DepthMaster: Unified Monocular Depth Estimation for Perspective and Panoramic Images
summary: While monocular depth estimation has achieved significant progress, achieving generalized metric depth estimation for both narrow field-of-view (FoV) perspectives and $360^\circ$ panoramas remains an unsolved challenge. Existing methods are often tailored to specific camera types and struggle to produce accurate metric depth that generalizes across diverse settings. This limitation stems from two key challenges: the inherent geometric discrepancy between perspective and panoramic cameras, and the scarcity of panoramic training data with metric annotations. In this work, we introduce DepthMaster, a unified metric depth estimation framework. Rather than employing specialized networks to learn spherical distortions, we reformulate the problem by decomposing panoramic images into overlapping perspective patches. Crucially, distinct from prior projection-based methods that rely on ad-hoc architectural modifications to handle boundaries, we introduce a novel Correspondence Consistency Loss (CCL) and inject virtual projection cameras as geometric priors, allowing us to seamlessly stitch the patches while avoiding specialized operators and keeping the backbone largely compatible with standard Transformer designs. This strategy also resolves the geometric differences by unifying all inputs into a canonical perspective representation, and effectively circumvents data scarcity by directly unlocking powerful metric priors from vast perspective datasets. Trained on a mixed dataset that contains only one panorama dataset, DepthMaster achieves state-of-the-art zero-shot performance on 13 diverse datasets, outperforming not only universal methods but also leading specialist models in both perspective and panoramic domains.

AI.tldr: DepthMaster presents a patch-based unified metric depth estimation for both perspective and 360° panoramas, using Correspondence Consistency Loss and virtual projection cameras to stitch overlapping patches and unify into a canonical perspective, yielding SOTA zero-shot results across 13 datasets trained on a single panorama dataset.
AI.motivation: Generalization gap in monocular metric depth across camera types due to perspective-panoramic geometry mismatch and limited panoramic metric data; need a method that leverages abundant perspective priors without bespoke spherical architectures.
AI.method: Reformulate: split panorama into overlapping perspective patches; introduce CCL to enforce cross-patch consistency; inject virtual projection cameras as priors; seamless stitching without specialized operators; backbone remains standard Transformer; inputs transformed to canonical perspective; training on mixed data with only one panorama dataset.
AI.result: State-of-the-art zero-shot on 13 datasets; outperforms universal methods and specialist models in both perspective and panoramic; demonstrates effective generalization and data efficiency.
AI.conclusion: Unified depth estimation across camera types via patch-based decomposition with geometric priors; resolves geometric differences and data scarcity; broad applicability to real-world cross-domain depth estimation.
